passage.title: Art Gallery Label Update
passage.text: The student gallery team wanted younger visitors to move through the art show more easily. At first, the team used stylish labels with long artist notes in tiny print, but students spent too much time reading the labels instead of following the path. Younger visitors said "I like the art, but I do not know which room is next." After hearing that, the team shortened each label to one easy title line and placed matching floor dots under important paintings. They also set a route check sign between the two busiest rooms so visitors could confirm the next room before turning. On the next test day, more students followed the art walk in the right order during the next event. The team noticed that clear route clues supported the artwork instead of competing with it. Because of that result, the gallery team planned to update the rest of the art walk with the same style.

questions.prompt: What is the passage mainly about?
choices: Painting a room with brighter colors
Buying more books for a library sale
Writing long reports about class rules
Improving art exhibit signs so younger visitors can follow the route
questions.rationale: The whole passage focuses on improving a system for younger students.

questions.prompt: What problem did students mention about the first plan?
choices: The first plan had too many steps
Students could not tell what to do next
Students spent too much time reading the labels
The labels were too small to read quickly
questions.rationale: The early part of the passage explains what did not work well.

questions.prompt: What did the team place under important paintings?
choices: Color blocks
Matching floor dots
Larger arrows
Short signs
questions.rationale: The middle of the passage tells what the team added or changed.

questions.prompt: What does competing mean in the seventh sentence?
choices: Trying to take attention away from something else
A room that is easy to clean
A rule about lunch time
A person who leads the school
questions.rationale: The word meaning comes from the way the passage uses it.

questions.prompt: Why did the new design work better for younger students?
choices: It made the room much larger
It reduced the number of students
It removed every sign from the area
The new route clues were easier to notice
questions.rationale: You have to connect the redesign changes with the later results.

questions.prompt: What will the team most likely do next?
choices: Remove every sign from the room
Ask students to memorize everything
Use the same clear route style for more art exhibits
Use the same clearer style in more places
questions.rationale: The last sentence points to the team's next likely action.
